chapter: Chapter 8: Force and Laws of Motion

questions.question: Explain the concept of force and its effects on motion.
questions.answer: Force is a push or pull acting on an object. It can change the speed, direction, or shape of an object.

questions.question: Differentiate between balanced and unbalanced forces with examples.
questions.answer: Balanced forces are equal and opposite forces that do not change motion, while unbalanced forces cause change in motion.

questions.question: Explain inertia and its types with examples.
questions.answer: Inertia is the tendency of an object to resist change in motion. Types include inertia of rest, motion and direction.

questions.question: State and explain Newton’s First Law of Motion with examples.
questions.answer: Newton’s First Law states that an object remains at rest or continues in uniform motion unless acted upon by an unbalanced force.

questions.question: Explain why Newton’s First Law is called the law of inertia.
questions.answer: Newton’s First Law explains inertia, which is the tendency of objects to resist changes in their state of motion.

questions.question: Define momentum and explain the factors affecting it.
questions.answer: Momentum is the product of mass and velocity. It depends on both mass and velocity.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between force, mass and acceleration.
questions.answer: According to Newton’s Second Law, force is directly proportional to acceleration and depends on mass.

questions.question: State and explain Newton’s Second Law of Motion.
questions.answer: The rate of change of momentum of an object is proportional to the applied force and occurs in the direction of the force.

questions.question: Define one Newton and explain the SI unit of force.
questions.answer: One Newton is the force required to produce acceleration of 1 m/s² in a mass of 1 kg.

questions.question: Explain Newton’s Third Law of Motion with examples.
questions.answer: For every action there is an equal and opposite reaction. These forces act on different objects.

questions.question: Explain the concept of action and reaction forces.
questions.answer: Action and reaction forces are equal in magnitude and opposite in direction and act on different bodies.

questions.question: Explain rocket propulsion using Newton’s Third Law.
questions.answer: Rockets move upward when gases are expelled downward, producing an equal and opposite reaction force.

questions.question: Explain the concept of friction and its effects.
questions.answer: Friction is the force that opposes motion between surfaces in contact.

questions.question: Explain the importance of friction in daily life.
questions.answer: Friction helps us walk, write and stop moving vehicles.

questions.question: Explain inertia of rest with a daily life example.
questions.answer: Inertia of rest is the tendency of an object to remain at rest unless acted upon by an external force.

questions.question: Explain inertia of motion with example.
questions.answer: Inertia of motion is the tendency of a moving object to continue moving.

questions.question: Explain inertia of direction with example.
questions.answer: Inertia of direction is the tendency of an object to resist change in direction.

questions.question: Explain why seat belts are necessary in vehicles.
questions.answer: Seat belts prevent passengers from moving forward due to inertia during sudden braking.

questions.question: Explain the importance of Newton’s laws in sports.
questions.answer: Newton’s laws explain motion in sports like running, jumping and throwing.

questions.question: Explain why heavier objects have greater inertia.
questions.answer: Objects with larger mass resist changes in motion more strongly.

questions.question: Explain why momentum increases with mass.
questions.answer: Momentum depends on mass, so increasing mass increases momentum.

questions.question: Explain why momentum increases with velocity.
questions.answer: Momentum is directly proportional to velocity according to p = mv.

questions.question: Explain the difference between mass and weight.
questions.answer: Mass is the amount of matter in an object while weight is the force due to gravity.

questions.question: Explain the importance of Newton’s laws in engineering.
questions.answer: Newton’s laws help design vehicles, machines and structures.

questions.question: Explain the contribution of Sir Isaac Newton to science.
questions.answer: Sir Isaac Newton formulated the three laws of motion that explain motion of objects.
